display.label: Hostname
display.description: Valid JDBC URL

display.label: User
display.description: Name of the database user to be used when connecting to the connection.

display.label: Password
display.description: Password of the database user to be used when connecting to the connection.

display.label: Connection pool minimum size
display.description: Minimum number of connection in the connection pool Default: 5

display.label: Connection pool maximum size
display.description: Maximum number of connection in the connection pool Default: 32

display.label: Connection pool timeout
display.description: Connection pool timeout Default: 1800

display.label: The insertion mode to use
display.description: The insertion mode to use. 'insert' - the default mode, uses standard SQL insert statements; 'upsert' - uses upsert semantics for the database if its supported and requires setting primary.key.mode and primary.key.fields;'update' - uses update semantics for the database if its supported. Default: insert

display.label: Controls whether records can be deleted by the connector
display.description: Whether to treat `null` record values as deletes. Requires primary.key.mode to be `record.key`. Default: false

display.label: Controls whether tables can be truncated by the connector
display.description: Whether to process debezium event `t` as truncate statement. Default: false

display.label: A format string for the collection name
display.description: A format string for the table, which may contain '${topic}' as a placeholder for the original topic name. Default: ${topic}

display.label: Primary key mode
display.description: The primary key mode. Default: none

display.label: Comma-separated list of primary key field names
display.description: A comma-separated list of primary key field names. This is interpreted differently depending on primary.key.mode.

display.label: Controls how schema evolution is handled by the sink connector
display.description: Controls how schema evolution is handled by the sink connector Default: none

display.label: Controls whether table, column, or other identifiers are quoted
display.description: When enabled, table, column, and other identifiers are quoted based on the database dialect. When disabled, only explicit cases where the dialect requires quoting will be used, such as names starting with an underscore. Default: false

display.label: CollectionNamingStrategy class
display.description: Name of the strategy class that defines the collection name from the topic name. It implements the CollectionNamingStrategy interface. Default: io.debezium.sink.naming.DefaultCollectionNamingStrategy

display.label: Name of the strategy class that implements the ColumnNamingStrategy interface
display.description: Name of the strategy class that implements the ColumnNamingStrategy interface. Default: io.debezium.connector.jdbc.naming.DefaultColumnNamingStrategy

display.label: Format string using table name
display.description: Alternative format that uses table name instead of topic name. Use ${schema} for schema name and ${table} for table name. Default: ${table}

display.label: The timezone used when inserting temporal values.
display.description: The timezone used when inserting temporal values. Defaults to UTC. Default: UTC

display.label: Name of the schema where postgis extension is installed
display.description: Name of the schema where postgis extension is installed. Default is public Default: public

display.label: Enable UNNEST-based batch inserts for PostgreSQL
display.description: When enabled, uses PostgreSQL UNNEST() for batch inserts which can significantly improve performance by reducing the number of SQL statements executed. This optimization is compatible with INSERT and UPSERT modes. Instead of executing multiple INSERT statements with JDBC batching, a single INSERT statement with UNNEST is used to insert all records at once. This can provide 5-10x performance improvement for high-throughput scenarios. Default: false

display.label: Allowing to insert explicit value for identity column in table for SQLSERVER.
display.description: Allowing to insert explicit value for identity column in table for SQLSERVER. Default: false

display.label: Specifies how many records to attempt to batch together into the destination table, when possible. You can also configure the connector’s underlying consumer’s max.poll.records using consumer.override.max.poll.records in the connector configuration.
display.description: Specifies how many records to attempt to batch together into the destination table, when possible. You can also configure the connector’s underlying consumer’s max.poll.records using consumer.override.max.poll.records in the connector configuration. Default: 500

display.label: Include Fields
display.description: A comma-separated list of regular expressions matching fully-qualified names of fields that should be included in change events. The field names must be delimited by the format <topic>:<field>

display.label: Exclude Fields
display.description: A comma-separated list of regular expressions matching fully-qualified names of fields that should be excluded from change events. The field names must be delimited by the format <topic>:<field>

display.label: Max retry count
display.description: Max retry count when fail to flush. In case of retriable exceptions, the flush will be retried for the defined max retry count. Default: 5

display.label: Delay to retry flush
display.description: Delay to retry when fail to flush Default: 1000

display.label: Restart connection on errors
display.description: Specifies whether the connector should attempt to restart the connection automatically upon connection-related errors. Defaults to false. In environments where the sink database uses asynchronous replication, enabling this option may risk data loss or inconsistencies during failover if the replica has not fully caught up with the primary. Default: false

display.label: CloudEvents messages schema name pattern
display.description: Regular expression pattern to identify CloudEvents messages by matching the schema name with this pattern. Default: .*CloudEvents\.Envelope$

groups.name: Filters
groups.description: Filtering options for tables and changes
